lesson: A leak at the dispense door is almost always the O-ring — a routine wear item, never the door or barrel seal. It is obvious enough that extra tests just burn job time you will want later.
symptoms: Mix leaking from the dispense door.

tests.inspect-beater: Door O-ring is flattened and cracked. That'll do it.

symptoms: Drip tray full of mix by lunchtime.
Dispense handle sticky no matter how often it's wiped.

tests.inspect-beater: Door O-ring flattened to a ribbon and split. The door itself is true and undamaged.

symptoms: Slow weep of mix from the door seam, worst right after each serve.

tests.inspect-beater: O-ring gone hard and cracked right around. Barrel seal bone dry — it's the door ring.

flavour: Sealed the deal for the price of a rubber band.
authenticityNote: Door O-rings are a routine wear item; leaks at the door are almost never anything else. The design trap is greed: players who run tests they don't need on a trivially obvious fault are wasting future job-time (GDD §2.2).
